notes: Gravity for THE COMFORTING VOID. The deep-work scenario — Elicia in the chair for hours, the Choose-Your-Own-Adventure episode finally taking shape. The cult audience refreshes the page. The phone is face-down. The room around the desk has, over the course of the night, ceased to register. This deck presses the MASTER REEL hardest — the work is happening — while DOUBT and STAGNATION are quieter than in the haunting scenario.

deck.title: the timeline scrolls itself
deck.flavor: you have, in the last forty minutes, not touched the mouse. the cursor still moves across the timeline as you read the project. the cursor is, you have always suspected, the muscle memory of the cuts.

deck.title: the master reel ticks
deck.flavor: the lower-right corner of the screen shows the project length. it has, in the last hour, grown by two minutes. you have, in the same hour, cut three.

deck.title: you watch the take fourteen times
deck.flavor: you set the in-point to the breath. you watch from there. you watch from the cut. you watch from the breath. by the fourteenth pass the breath is the take.

deck.title: you cut three frames
deck.flavor: the three frames are the difference. the version with them runs at the old length. the version without them is the version.

deck.title: anya holds the breath
deck.flavor: in the rendered preview. the cut from the breath to the next clip is, you note, half a second cleaner than it was last hour.

deck.title: the cult audience refreshes the page
deck.flavor: the forum thread shows the small counter increment that means somebody is checking. fourteen visitors logged in the last hour. you do not, today, click through.

deck.title: the render takes nine minutes
deck.flavor: the laptop fan rises to the high pitch. you stand up. you do not, this turn, leave the room. you stand at the desk and watch the progress bar.

deck.title: you stand up to walk and don't
deck.flavor: you push the chair back. you take half a step. you sit back down. the work, you note, is here.

deck.title: the laptop fan kicks in
deck.flavor: from the render. it is, you note, the same fan-pitch you mixed into the audio of episode IX. the fan is, technically, the show.

deck.title: you eat a cookie from the box on the shelf
deck.flavor: the nice ones somebody gave you in '23. one cookie. you put the lid back. the box has, you note, exactly six left.

deck.title: you watch your own cold open
deck.flavor: from the master file of episode 0. you in the b&w chair, nodding once. the host does not speak. the host does not, today, look at you any differently than she usually looks.

deck.title: the headphones have, in two years, formed to your skull
deck.flavor: you take them off to scratch your ear. you put them back on. the seal is, you note, the seal.

deck.title: the episode opens with the wide shot you didn't film
deck.flavor: in your head. you frame the shot from the roof — the one you decided, today, not to actually film. you cut to it in your head. the cut works.

deck.title: you lock the cut
deck.flavor: you set the playhead at the in. you set it at the out. you save the project. you do not, this turn, watch it back.

deck.title: the choose-your-own-adventure episode has shape
deck.flavor: you watch the assembly cut. it works. not finished — but, for the first time, the episode is a thing. it has, in your private accounting, the shape of an episode rather than the shape of a timeline.
